title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: July 03, 2026: U.S. Markets Closed for Independence Day Observed

items.headline: U.S. Stock Markets Closed for Independence Day Observed; Trading Resumes Monday
items.summary: U.S. equity markets, including the NYSE and Nasdaq, are closed today, Friday, July 3, 2026, in observance of the Independence Day holiday. Trading will resume on Monday, July 6, 2026. The last trading session on Thursday, July 2, saw the Dow Jones Industrial Average hit a new record high, while the S&P 500 remained largely flat and the Nasdaq Composite declined due to a sell-off in technology and semiconductor stocks. Investor sentiment was influenced by a weaker-than-expected June jobs report, which eased concerns about further Federal Reserve interest rate hikes.

items.headline: Apple Rises on Strong Performance
items.summary: Apple ($AAPL) shares gained 4.8%, significantly contributing to the Dow's advance.

items.headline: McDonald's Also Contributes to Dow's Strength
items.summary: McDonald's ($MCD) saw a 4.2% increase, being the Dow's second-best performer.

items.headline: Tesla Drops Despite Strong Deliveries
items.summary: Tesla ($TSLA) ended sharply lower, dropping 7.5%, despite reporting second-quarter vehicle deliveries that exceeded market expectations.

items.headline: Micron Continues Decline Amid Chip Sell-off
items.summary: Micron Technology ($MU) erased early gains to drop 5.5% on Thursday, extending losses from the previous day, as chipmakers faced renewed investor scrutiny over valuations.

items.headline: Nvidia Shares Fall Amid AI Stock Pressure
items.summary: Nvidia ($NVDA) fell 1.4% as semiconductor and AI-related stocks experienced profit-taking and concerns over valuations.

sections.title: Market News & Analysis (Last Trading Day: July 2, 2026)

items.headline: Weaker Jobs Report Eases Rate Hike Concerns
items.summary: The June U.S. employment report, released on Thursday, showed the economy added a softer-than-expected 57,000 jobs, falling short of economists' forecasts. This softer labor market data reduced investor fears that the Federal Reserve would need to raise interest rates again, leading to a scaling back of expectations for a September rate hike.

items.headline: Sector Rotation Benefits Value Stocks, Tech Under Pressure
items.summary: Thursday's trading session saw a notable rotation as investors shifted from high-flying technology and AI stocks, which dragged down the Nasdaq, towards more value-oriented sectors. Financials, Industrials, Healthcare, and Consumer stocks benefited from the reduced pressure on interest rates, suggesting a broadening of market leadership.

items.headline: AI Stocks Face Valuation Scrutiny
items.summary: Semiconductor and AI-related stocks, including Nvidia, AMD, Broadcom, and Micron, faced pressure due to renewed concerns over valuations and AI infrastructure spending after extraordinary gains throughout 2026.
